codexExhaustedMode.trigger: Set codexAllowanceMode to exhausted when Codex weekly allowance is gone or Josh explicitly requests outage continuity.
codexExhaustedMode.rule: Route dashboard-safe summaries, reviews, digests, stale-task compression, and non-execution synthesis to Gemini. Route public current-events, X-native research, outside critique, and batch audits to xAI. Route explicit non-sensitive fallback checks to OpenRouter. Reserve Codex, OpenAI API, or OpenRouter spend for execution, private connector work, repo edits, terminal actions, approvals, and final integration.
codexExhaustedMode.telegramDisclosure: Josh 2.0 and JAIMES must state Active Model/Auth and Route reason before doing substantive work while this mode is active.

policy.rule: Deterministic ladder first: Gemini Flash is the dashboard-safe front-desk/router-assist lane, Flash Lite is bounded worker-only and never a router, Gemini Pro is judgment/escalation, JAIMES/Codex owns verified execution, J.A.I.N/OpenClaw is fallback/staging, and Josh approves irreversible external actions.
policy.tripleConstraint: Balance speed, routing effectiveness, and token efficiency by task context: mobile/frontdesk favors speed, complex/high-risk work favors route quality, ambient background work favors local/Gemini efficiency.
policy.failoverRule: Before external fallback, keep ownership on the right internal lane. If Gemini/xAI/OpenRouter fails for dashboard-safe work, preserve a bounded state package and continue through Codex/OpenAI on the selected owner lane.
policy.statePreservation: Failover packages may include objective summary, current step, recent safe activity, route decision, task/handoff ids, AGENTS.md/shared-skill pointers, and sanitized memory refs. They must exclude secrets, OAuth payloads, cookies, passwords, tokens, raw emails, raw connector data, and private account contents.
policy.lastModeChangeReason: enable Gemini-first orchestration and conserve Codex weekly allowance

providers.label: Codex / OpenAI
providers.lastRouteReason: Subscription-backed execution lane; token estimates are telemetry, not incremental billable spend.

providers.label: Gemini
providers.role: Front-desk/review/judgment ladder: Flash for fast safe triage, Pro for escalation, Lite worker-only.
providers.lastRouteReason: Subscription-backed Gemini lane; use for dashboard-safe review and summaries without showing scary token-equivalent costs.

providers.label: xAI / Grok
providers.role: Current-events, X-native research, outside critique, and batch audit specialist
providers.lastRouteReason: X Premium/Grok subscription-backed lane for current-events and X-native context; not treated as usage-metered by default.
providers.usageAllowance: X Premium/Grok subscription; API calls tracked separately if used.

providers.label: OpenRouter
providers.role: Fallback route when primary lanes are unavailable or a specific outside model is needed
providers.lastRouteReason: Fallback only; prefer Codex, Gemini, or xAI specialist lanes first.
providers.usageAllowance: Metered fallback only; hidden unless active or spending.

providers.label: Ollama Pro
providers.role: Local/Ollama Cloud model lane; GLM-5.2 eval plus local models.
providers.lastRouteReason: $200/year amortized as $16.67/mo and $4.17/wk; local models remain zero incremental cost.
providers.usageAllowance: Ollama Pro paid annually; cloud/local usage shown as subscription capacity, not scary token-equivalent spend.

guardrails: Do not route secrets, OAuth payloads, raw connector data, raw emails, or private account contents to Gemini, xAI, or OpenRouter automatically.
Any provider with a daily cap is blocked once dailySpendUsd is greater than or equal to dailyCapUsd unless Josh explicitly approves an override.
When Codex allowance mode is exhausted, non-execution dashboard-safe work must leave Codex first; Codex/API/OpenRouter spend is reserved for execution and private actions.
xAI is not the default brain. It is a specialist lane for current-events, X-native research, outside critique, and batch audits.
Gemini Flash Lite must never be the sole gatekeeper for escalation.
Execution tasks involving code, files, tools, crons, tests, browser/auth work, trade prep, or account mutation must route to Codex/JAIMES/JOSHeX approval lanes, not Lite/Flash alone.
Route telemetry must not contain raw prompts, secrets, OAuth payloads, cookies, passwords, tokens, raw emails, or private account contents.
billingBasis: Codex $200/mo, Gemini $20/mo, Grok/X Premium ~$8/mo, Ollama Pro $200/year ($16.67/mo, $4.17/wk). Fixed subscriptions; token/session estimates are telemetry only.
